summary: Matched the common fighter cliff escape action by moving ftCo_8009B040 and the CliffEscape Anim/IASA/Phys/Coll callbacks out of asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s into a new src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_CliffEscape.c/.h pair. The matched implementation selects quick versus slow cliff escape based on fighter damage percent compared to p_ftCommonData->x488, changes motion state, initializes animation and common flags, then delegates physics/collision behavior to existing cliff catch/climb helpers. The PR report showed 100.00% for the new file and all listed functions. No review discussion was present in the provided context.
what_changed: Deleted the assembly implementation of ftCo_8009B040, ftCo_CliffEscape_Anim, ftCo_CliffEscape_IASA, ftCo_CliffEscape_Phys, and ftCo_CliffEscape_Coll from asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s.
Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_CliffEscape.c with a matched C implementation of the cliff escape entry function and its action callbacks.
Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_CliffEscape.h declaring ftCo_8009B040 and the CliffEscape callbacks with address comments.
Registered ftCo_CliffEscape.c in obj_files.mk immediately after the related CliffWait, CliffClimb, and CliffAttack common fighter files.
Removed an outdated ftCo_8009B040 declaration from src/melee/ft/ft_08A4.h and removed a duplicated/centralized ftCo_CliffClimb_Anim declaration from src/melee/ft/ft_0877.h.
Added includes so related common cliff files can reference the newly separated headers, including ftCo_CliffEscape.h in ftCo_CliffAttack.c and ftCo_CliffClimb.h in ftCo_Init.c.
Removed the local sdata2 constants formerly used by the asm version for NULL, 1.0, and the integer-to-double conversion constant associated with the 176.0 comparison.
smart_moves: Kept CliffEscape in its own ftCommon source/header pair, matching the existing per-action organization used for CliffWait, CliffClimb, and CliffAttack.
Expressed the motion-state choice as a typed FtMotionId ternary: damage percent below p_ftCommonData->x488 selects ftCo_MS_CliffEscapeQuick, otherwise ftCo_MS_CliffEscapeSlow.

Reused existing CliffClimb callbacks for Anim, Phys, and Coll rather than duplicating shared behavior, mirroring the original assembly wrappers that simply called ftCo_CliffClimb_Anim, ftCo_CliffClimb_Phys, and ftCo_CliffClimb_Coll.
Represented the no-op IASA callback as an empty C function, matching the original single blr function body.
Used named bitfields fp->x221D_b7 and fp->x221D_b5 instead of raw byte/rlwimi manipulation, making the flag side effects clearer while preserving behavior.
Added @todo notes identifying shared code with ftCo_8009AEA4 / ftCo_8009AB9C, signaling a future refactor opportunity without disturbing matching.
decomp_lessons: Small action callback wrappers can often be matched cleanly by delegating directly to an existing sibling action's callback rather than forcing artificial local logic.

When assembly uses xoris/lfd conversion to compare an integer common-data threshold against a float damage percent, a direct C comparison against p_ftCommonData fields may still match if typed carefully.
Replacing raw motion-state constants with FtMotionId enum names improves readability and documents action semantics once the matching relationship is known.
Action-specific headers are preferable to accumulating declarations in broad ft_08A4.h or ft_0877.h headers once a module has been split out.
Build-order placement in obj_files.mk matters for decomp slices: the new C object was inserted near adjacent cliff action objects and before the remaining ft_08A4.s object.
Preserve address comments in new headers for searchable symbol-to-address mapping during ongoing decomp work.
naming_conventions: Common fighter cliff escape files follow the ftCo_CliffEscape.c and ftCo_CliffEscape.h naming pattern.
Callbacks use the action suffix convention ftCo_CliffEscape_Anim, ftCo_CliffEscape_IASA, ftCo_CliffEscape_Phys, and ftCo_CliffEscape_Coll.
The unresolved/action entry function kept its address-derived name ftCo_8009B040 rather than inventing a semantic name.
The header guard used the address-style form GALE01_09B040, matching the function address region.
Typed aliases ftCo_GObj, ftCo_Fighter, and FtMotionId were used in the new source instead of generic Fighter_GObj or raw integer types.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: The removed assembly selected between constants 0x103 and 0x102; the C equivalent mapped these to ftCo_MS_CliffEscapeQuick and ftCo_MS_CliffEscapeSlow.
Fighter_ChangeMotionState was matched with arguments gobj, msid, Ft_MF_None, NULL, 0, 1, 0, corresponding to the original f1 NULL/0.0, f2 1.0, f3 0.0 and zero integer flags.
The assembly wrappers around CliffClimb callbacks were matched as direct one-call C wrappers, avoiding unnecessary stack/local artifacts.
The bit updates at fighter offset 0x221D were represented through named bitfields x221D_b7 and x221D_b5.
The asm-local sdata2 constants for 0.0/NULL, 1.0, and the 0x4330 conversion pattern disappeared once the logic was expressed in C.
The PR's generated report showed 0 score out of max for the file and all listed functions, i.e. 100.00% matching.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR context.
The only explicit human/body evidence was the matching report for src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_CliffEscape.c showing 100.00% for the file and five functions.
follow_up_queries: Look for future PRs that resolve the @todo shared code with ftCo_8009AEA4 or ftCo_8009AB9C.
Check adjacent cliff action modules for similar wrapper callbacks that can be moved from ft_08A4.s into action-specific C files.
Search for remaining declarations in broad ft_08A4.h or ft_0877.h that should migrate to action-specific headers.
Investigate p_ftCommonData->x488 naming once common fighter data fields are better understood.
